hypotheses.claim: The manuscript's text structure reflects a mix of prose and verse, with the herbal section being primarily composed of short, rhyming couplets.
evidence_for: Herbal section has higher entropy levels compared to other sections
Word-final glyph distribution in herbal section shows a possible rhyme scheme
evidence_against: Other sections do not show similar patterns
hypotheses.test: Chi-squared test for independence between word-final glyphs and section type
tests_remaining: Chi-squared test for independence between word-final glyphs and section type
hypotheses.reasoning: If the herbal section is primarily composed of short, rhyming couplets, we would expect to see a significant correlation between word-final glyphs and section type.

hypotheses.claim: The text uses a combination of substitution and transposition ciphers in the biological section.
evidence_for: Biological section has higher entropy levels compared to other sections
Word-final glyph distribution in biological section shows possible patterns consistent with transposition
evidence_against: Other sections do not show similar patterns
hypotheses.test: Mutual information analysis between word-final glyphs and word positions
tests_remaining: Mutual information analysis between word-final glyphs and word positions
hypotheses.reasoning: If the biological section uses a combination of substitution and transposition ciphers, we would expect to see significant correlations between word-final glyphs and word positions.

hypotheses.claim: The Currier A/B split reflects a genuine linguistic difference between two distinct languages or dialects.
evidence_for: Currier A has higher hapax ratio compared to Currier B
Word-final glyph distribution in Currier A is more consistent with Latin word endings
evidence_against: Other sections do not show similar patterns
hypotheses.test: Kullback-Leibler divergence between word-final glyph distributions of Currier A and B
tests_remaining: Kullback-Leibler divergence between word-final glyph distributions of Currier A and B
hypotheses.reasoning: If the Currier A/B split reflects a genuine linguistic difference, we would expect to see significant differences in word-final glyph distributions between the two sections.

hypotheses.claim: The text is encoded using a simple substitution cipher on an unknown language.
evidence_for: Glyph entropy levels are lower than expected for Latin or Italian
Word-final glyph distribution does not match Latin or Italian word endings
evidence_against: Other sections do not show similar patterns
hypotheses.test: Chi-squared test for independence between word-final glyphs and language model predictions
tests_remaining: Chi-squared test for independence between word-final glyphs and language model predictions
hypotheses.reasoning: If the text is encoded using a simple substitution cipher on an unknown language, we would expect to see significant correlations between word-final glyphs and language model predictions.

hypotheses.claim: The manuscript's text structure reflects a single, coherent narrative.
evidence_for: Word entropy levels are consistent across sections
Zipf exponent is moderate, indicating possible narrative structure
evidence_against: Other sections do not show similar patterns
hypotheses.reasoning: If the manuscript's text structure reflects a single, coherent narrative, we would expect to see significant correlations between word positions and topics.
